Release Management Process

El siguiente personal firma de conformidad el documento "E – KA – GL013 – IT – PRO – Release Management Process", con fecha de última actualización "Octubre de 2023" y vigencia a partir de su publicación. El presente documento deja sin efecto cualquier otra normatividad anterior relativa al tema que trata y permanecerá vigente hasta una nueva emisión debidamente autorizada que la reforme, adicione o derogue total o parcialmente.

Contenido

1. Objective 2. Scope 3. Team Involved 4. Interrelated Processes 5. Glossary 6. Process Description Flowcharts Minimum Requirements for Deployments in Production Scripts Changes Approvers Hotfix Steps to Document a Change in Production Deployment Execution Rollback 7. Controls 8. References

Visión general

This document establishes the Release Management process for Kavak Holding and its subsidiaries, defining the steps, responsibilities, and controls for deploying changes to the NetSuite production environment. It aims to ensure a consistent, controlled approach to change management, safeguarding system integrity and business continuity across all regions through proper documentation, testing, approvals, and segregation of duties.

Control documental

1. Objective

The purpose of this document is to explain the Release Management process, which includes supporting documentation, tests, user acceptance, approvals, segregation of duties and environments, monitoring phase and rollbacks.

2. Scope

This process applies to all changes deployed to the NetSuite Production environment, covering all countries and instances managed by Kavak Holding and its subsidiaries.

3. Team Involved

Business Users, Developers, Functional Teams, Product Management, Chapter Leads, NetSuite Administration.

4. Interrelated Processes

  • PD-GL-011 – Deployment in Production Environment
  • PD-GL-012 – Monthly Audits New Releases/Deployments

5. Glossary

Term Definition
Deployments Includes changes in scripts.
Production Environment NetSuite Production – all countries / instances.
Hotfix A correction that needs to be released as quickly as possible.
Release Management Change Management.
USO INTERNO Versión: 1.3 Última revisión: Oct 2023 3

6. Process Description

Flowcharts

Minimum Requirements for Deployments in Production – NetSuite

Regular Deployments in Production Environment (exception: Hotfixes, whose requirements are explained separately in this document) must be supported according to the following steps:

  1. Ticket in Jira to support the request.
  2. Functional Documentation (system behavior) and, if applicable, Technical Documentation (system configuration, script changes).
  3. Test Matrix / Test Cases (referenced to Jira Ticket Number – can be supported in a separate document).
  4. UAT: User Acceptance Test (evidence must be supported by an e-mail or User's OK as a comment in the ticket).

Scripts Changes

If a deployment involves a SuiteScript whose impact is changing, adding, or removing a functionality, the defined script change process must be followed. Changes must NOT be executed through a script change in the file cabinet.

Approvers

Employees who are allowed to approve a Merge Request to Production:

Role Approvers
Script MR Approvers Octavio Manzano, Rafael Natera, Ivan Macias, Einis Rodriguez, Omar Ortiz, Wilmer Perez, Gonzalo Rodriguez
Manual Configuration (non-script) Frewuill Rodriguez, Aneriz Rodriguez

Note: GitLab is configured to prevent self-approvals. This must be restricted at a project level via the "Approval Settings" screen.

New Fields requests, Custom Records, Forms, etc. that do not require scripts and where manual bundling cannot be carried out (SDF or Bundle), can only be executed by: Frewuill Rodriguez, Aneriz Rodriguez.

USO INTERNO Versión: 1.3 Última revisión: Oct 2023 4

Hotfix

Ticket must be in place, but functional and technical documentation are optional.

Employees allowed to approve Hotfix MR: Einis Rodriguez, Uriel Ordoñez, Octavio Manzano.

Changes in Platform Configuration can only be executed by: Aneriz Rodriguez, Frewuill Rodriguez.

Response Times

Merge Requests must be submitted to MR Approvers (see Approvers section above) with at least 5 working days in advance of the expected deployment date.

Note: This is critical mainly for Developers who do not belong to the ERP team nor report to any MR Approver.

Steps to Document a Change in Production

A. Receiving the Change Request

The process begins when the User raises a ticket in Jira. The following minimum information must be included:

  1. Type of change
  2. Reason
  3. Business Impact / KPIs impact
  4. Expected Deployment Date
  5. Departments/Users impacted
  6. Request Details (brief description of the solution)

This must be approved or rejected by the User's supervisor. Approval can be supported by e-mail or by adding 'OK' as a comment in the ticket.

B. Functional and Technical Documentation

The following must be included in the documentation:

  1. Priority and justification
  2. Business Process
  3. Product Owner
  4. Problem Description
  5. Proposed Solution

C. Test Matrix / Test Cases

Documentation should include:

  • Transaction numbers generated for testing purposes
  • Test results for each case
  • Print screens
  • Users (Name, Email, Job Title) who participated in testing

Tests must be executed in QA environment (not production).

D. User Acceptance Test

After testing is concluded, the User who requested the change must approve the deployment based on test results. Acceptance can be obtained via e-mail attached to the Jira ticket, or the user can provide OK/Approval as a comment. This must be obtained prior to deployment in production.

USO INTERNO Versión: 1.3 Última revisión: Oct 2023 5

Deployment Execution

The MR Approver receives the deployment request and reviews supporting documentation. They must receive the request at least 5 working days prior to the expected deployment date.

The approver can approve or reject the request based on the following criteria (examples):

  • Lack of Test Evidence
  • Lack of UAT
  • No Business Impact documented
  • Incomplete documentation

If the MR is approved, the approver executes the deployment on the defined date.

Rollback

After deployment in production is executed, the functional and technical team must monitor for any issues affecting the production environment. The suggested monitoring period is 3 working days.

If, after the Monitoring Phase, it is confirmed that the business process is in accordance with the solution, the ticket is closed. This confirmation must be added as a comment on the Jira ticket.

7. Controls

  • Segregation of duties between Developers and Chapter Leads.
  • All changes are approved prior to execution in Production.
  • Permissions: Setup/SuiteScripts was adjusted to View level in all roles except for the NetSuite Chapter Lead (MR Approvers' Role), since deployments require this level of permission. However, all steps described in this procedure must be followed. (Exception: Hotfixes only – NSD-11556).

8. References

  • Release Management Reference Document
  • NSD-11556 – SuiteScript Permission Configuration
